methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
DIRAC is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Fil Aronshtein that has raised $5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable, though same-stage peers suitable for direct median comparison are limited.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation lens. Using the $5M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $12M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while 12 same-sector companies exist, filtering to same-stage and similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making median-based triangulation unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, centered on $12M. This estimate carries meaningful uncertainty. The single-method foundation (stage progression) provides directional rather than definitive guidance; absence of tight-fit peer comparables limits the ability to calibrate for company-specific execution risk, competitive positioning within robotics and manufacturing, or burn profile relative to staged deployment of capital. Additional data on near-term milestones and follow-on round signals would strengthen conviction in this range.
